2565	\label{sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality}
2566	
2567	The relative Picard consumer (\cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor}) re-bases its
2568	line-bundle carrier onto the \(\otimes\)-invertibility predicate
2569	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}, and its
2570	functorial structure maps are \emph{pullback} maps of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules: the
2571	projection pullback \(\pi_T^*\) along \(C \times_S T \to T\) and the base-change
2572	pullback along \(C \times_S T' \to C \times_S T\). For these maps to descend to the
2573	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) carrier one needs that \emph{pullback preserves
2574	\(\otimes\)-invertibility} for \emph{general} morphisms of schemes --- the
2575	projection and the base-change morphism are neither open immersions nor flat.
2576	
2577	This is a genuinely different fact from the open-immersion compatibility
2578	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, and not a corollary of it. That lemma handles
2579	restriction along an open immersion, and it does so by routing through the
2580	\emph{lax right} adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}/\mathtt{restrictScalars}\), which is
2581	strong monoidal there \emph{only} because an open immersion's structure-sheaf ring
2582	map is a local \emph{isomorphism} (\(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)). A general scheme
2583	morphism has no such isomorphism, so the right-adjoint route is unavailable.
2584	
2585	The general case is the \emph{left} adjoint, pullback \(f^* = \) inverse image
2586	\(=(\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\). Conceptually pullback \emph{is}
2587	``extension of scalars'', and extension of scalars along any ring map \emph{is} strong
2588	monoidal --- the base-change tensorator \((M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S \cong
2589	(M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S)\) is an isomorphism with no flatness
2590	hypothesis. So at the level of mathematics there is no obstacle: pullback commutes
2591	with \(\otimes\) for every morphism. The one genuine subtlety is purely structural to
2592	the formalisation: the project carries \(f^*\) \emph{abstractly}, as the left adjoint
2593	\((\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\), so that \((f^*).\mathrm{obj}\) has
2594	no sectionwise value to which one could literally attach the \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\)
2595	tensorator. The resolution is the standard one for an abstract left adjoint: build the
2596	strong-monoidal structure on a \emph{concrete} model \(P\) of the pullback and transport
2597	it to \(f^*\) along the uniqueness of left adjoints. This dissolves the
2598	``no sectionwise formula'' worry entirely --- one never computes \(f^*\) sectionwise;
2599	one transports a concrete monoidal structure across an adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism.
2600	This is exactly the device that closed \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
2601	
2602	It is worth recording why two tempting shortcuts are not taken. First, one might hope
2603	that since pullback is \emph{some} (op)lax monoidal functor, invertible objects are
2604	preserved for free. This is \emph{false}: global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal,
2605	yet \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not an invertible module, so a mere
2606	oplax comparison map does \emph{not} suffice --- one genuinely has to prove the tensor
2607	comparison is an \emph{isomorphism}. Second, one might route through local triviality,
2608	``invertible \(\Rightarrow\) locally \(\mathcal{O}\) \(\Rightarrow\) pulls back to
2609	locally \(\mathcal{O}\)''. This is a mirage on the present carrier: recovering
2610	\(\mathtt{IsInvertible}\) from local triviality is the reverse bridge
2611	\(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial} \Rightarrow \mathtt{IsInvertible}\), i.e.\ exactly the
2612	dual/tensor-inverse manufacture that the invertibility-carrier pivot
2613	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier}) was built to delete. Restricting to \emph{flat}
2614	structure maps does not help either: flatness governs the exactness of \(f^*\), not
2615	whether the tensor comparison is an isomorphism (it is an isomorphism for \emph{every}
2616	morphism, flat or not). The strong-monoidal pullback route is therefore primary
2617	precisely because it pulls back the existing tensor-inverse witness directly, through a
2618	comparison isomorphism valid for all morphisms, never reintroducing the deleted dual.
2619	
2620	\begin{lemma}
2621	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ for a general scheme morphism]
2622	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso}
2623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIso}
2624	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
2625	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
2626	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
2627	  Writing \(f^* = \mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f\) for the inverse-image functor
2628	  of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules, the canonical comparison
2629	  \[
2630	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2631	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2632	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N)
2633	  \]
2634	  is an isomorphism, natural in \(M\) and \(N\). No local-freeness, flatness, or
2635	  open-immersion hypothesis is required: it holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
2636	  and all morphisms \(f\).
2637	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2638	  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a
2639	  % morphism of ringed spaces commutes with tensor product, functorially)
2640	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400).
2641	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2642	  % "\begin{lemma}
2643	  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
2644	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
2645	  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
2646	  % be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
2647	  % $f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
2648	  % = f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$
2649	  % functorially in $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$.
2650	  % \end{lemma}"
2651	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
2652	  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}: for a morphism of ringed spaces
2653	  \(f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)\) and \(\mathcal{O}_Y\)-modules
2654	  \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\), one has \(f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes \mathcal{G}) =
2655	  f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes f^*\mathcal{G}\) functorially.}
2656	\end{lemma}
2657	
2658	\begin{proof}
2659	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
2660	  Mathematically, pullback is extension of scalars and extension of scalars is strong
2661	  monoidal, so the comparison is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The only work is to
2662	  carry this through the formalisation's \emph{abstract} pullback \(f^* =
2663	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\), which has no sectionwise value. We
2664	  do not compute \(f^*\) sectionwise; instead we equip a \emph{concrete} model of the
2665	  pullback with a strong-monoidal structure and transport it to \(f^*\) along the
2666	  uniqueness of left adjoints. This mirrors Mathlib's own construction of the
2667	  extension-of-scalars monoidal structure in
2668	  \texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean}, where the
2669	  \emph{left} adjoint's strong-monoidal structure is built explicitly (via the
2670	  base-change distributivity isomorphism) and only afterwards is the right adjoint's
2671	  lax structure derived from the adjunction --- never the reverse direction. The
2672	  argument has three steps.
2673	
2674	  \emph{Step 1 (a concrete strong-monoidal pullback \(P\)).} Build the concrete functor
2675	  \[
2676	    P \;=\; \mathtt{sheafification} \;\circ\; (\text{presheaf base change}),
2677	  \]
2678	  the presheaf-level inverse image (sectionwise extension of scalars along the
2679	  structure-sheaf ring maps of \(f\)) followed by sheafification on the small Zariski
2680	  site --- the same \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) shape already used in
2681	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. Equip \(P\) with a strong-monoidal structure. Its
2682	  tensorator is the linear-algebra strong-monoidal core
2683	  \(\mathtt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange}\),
2684	  \[
2685	    (M \otimes_R N) \otimes_R S
2686	      \;\cong\;
2687	    (M \otimes_R S) \otimes_S (N \otimes_R S),
2688	  \]
2689	  an isomorphism for \emph{every} ring map \(R \to S\), with no flatness, sheafified
2690	  pointwise; this is precisely Mathlib's \((\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi).\mathtt{Monoidal}\)
2691	  lifted to sheaves. The unit comparison is the already-landed sheafification-monoidality
2692	  brick \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\). Thus \(P\) is strong monoidal with each
2693	  tensorator and the unit comparison an isomorphism, with no hypothesis on \(f\).
2694	
2695	  \emph{Step 2 (\(P\) is left adjoint to pushforward).} Exhibit an adjunction
2696	  \(P \dashv \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforward}\,f\). This is the universal property
2697	  of sheafified extension of scalars: the presheaf base change is left adjoint to
2698	  presheaf restriction of scalars, and sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion,
2699	  so the composite \(P\) is left adjoint to the pushforward.
2700	
2701	  \emph{Step 3 (transport to the abstract pullback).} By definition
2702	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f = (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathtt{leftAdjoint}\)
2703	  is \emph{a} left adjoint of the pushforward. By the uniqueness of left adjoints
2704	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) --- the same device that closed
2705	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} --- there is a canonical natural isomorphism
2706	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.pullback}\,f \cong P\). The strong-monoidal structure of
2707	  \(P\) travels along this isomorphism, so its tensorator becomes a genuine
2708	  \emph{isomorphism}
2709	  \[
2710	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
2711	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2712	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
2713	  \]
2714	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\). This is the asserted comparison; the abstract pullback is
2715	  never evaluated sectionwise --- the monoidal structure is transported across the
2716	  adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism.
2717	
2718	  Two shortcuts are deliberately avoided. The bundled doctrinal adjunction
2719	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\) is present at the pinned commit, but
2720	  it is not usable here: it requires \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{SheafOfModules})\)
2721	  instances (the project carries \(\otimes_X\) as a bespoke sheafified tensor with no
2722	  such instance and no \(\mathtt{pushforward.LaxMonoidal}\)), and in any case it would
2723	  yield only an \emph{oplax} comparison \emph{map}, not the required isomorphism. And a
2724	  mere oplax map does not suffice: an (op)lax monoidal functor does \emph{not} preserve
2725	  invertible objects for free --- global sections \(\Gamma\) is lax monoidal yet
2726	  \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^1, \mathcal{O}(1)) = 0\) is not invertible --- so proving the
2727	  comparison is an isomorphism, as Step~3 does, is genuinely necessary.
2728	
2729	  The full strong-monoidal packaging of \(f^*\) is delivered by the transported
2730	  structure \(P.\mathtt{Monoidal}\); the invertibility consumer
2731	  \cref{lem:isinvertible_pullback} consumes only the comparison \emph{isomorphism} at
2732	  its witness pair, which Step~3 supplies.
2733	\end{proof}
2734	
2735	\begin{lemma}
2736	
2737	\leanok
2738	[Composition coherence of the pushforward unit comparison]
2739	  \label{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
2740	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
2741	  Let \(h : Z \to Y\) and \(f : Y \to X\) be composable morphisms of schemes. Write
2742	  \(\mathrm{upu}\) for the pushforward (right-adjoint) unit comparison
2743	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\), the canonical map
2744	  \(\mathcal{O} \to f_*(\mathcal{O})\) comparing the structure sheaf of the target with
2745	  the pushforward of the structure sheaf of the source. Then \(\mathrm{upu}\) satisfies
2746	  the composition coherence
2747	  \[
2748	    \mathrm{upu}(h \mathbin{;} f)
2749	      \;=\;
2750	    \mathrm{upu}\,f \;\mathbin{;}\;
2751	      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathrm{upu}\,h\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
2752	      (\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{hom},
2753	  \]
2754	  where \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f : (h \mathbin{;} f)_* \cong f_* \circ h_*\) is the
2755	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism of the pushforward. Sectionwise both sides are nothing
2756	  but the functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps, so the identity holds on the
2757	  nose after the comparison of sections. This is the right-adjoint half of the unit
2758	  composition coherence, and it is the input consumed by the mate transport that produces
2759	  the pullback-side coherence \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.
2760	\end{lemma}
2761	
2762	\begin{proof}
2763	  Both sides are morphisms of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules; it suffices to compare them on
2764	  sections over each open. Each comparison \(\mathrm{upu}\) acts on sections as the
2765	  structure-sheaf ring homomorphism induced by the relevant morphism of schemes, and the
2766	  pseudofunctor coherence \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) acts as the identification of
2767	  \((h \mathbin{;} f)\)-pushforward sections with iterated \(f_*\,h_*\)-pushforward
2768	  sections. The composition coherence is therefore the functoriality identity
2769	  \((h \mathbin{;} f)^\sharp = h^\sharp \circ f^\sharp\) of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
2770	  which holds definitionally; the two sides agree section by section.
2771	\end{proof}

2773	\begin{lemma}
2774	
2775	\leanok
2776	[Composition coherence of the pullback unit comparison]
2777	  \label{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
2778	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
2779	  \uses{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
2780	  Let \(h : Z \to Y\) and \(f : Y \to X\) be composable morphisms of schemes. Write
2781	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\) for the pullback (left-adjoint) unit comparison
2782	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\), the canonical map
2783	  \(f^*\mathcal{O} \to \mathcal{O}\). Then \(\mathrm{pbu}\) satisfies the composition
2784	  coherence
2785	  \[
2786	    \mathrm{pbu}(h \mathbin{;} f)
2787	      \;=\;
2788	    (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv} \;\mathbin{;}\;
2789	      (\mathtt{pullback}\,h).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
2790	      \mathrm{pbu}\,h,
2791	  \]
2792	  where \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f : (h \mathbin{;} f)^* \cong h^* \circ f^*\) is the
2793	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphism of the pullback. This is the genuinely-new ingredient
2794	  for \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}: the left-adjoint pullback is defined abstractly, with
2795	  no sectionwise value, so this identity is \emph{not} a sectionwise statement and cannot
2796	  be checked on sections.
2797	\end{lemma}
2798	
2799	\begin{proof}
2800	  \uses{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}
2801	  The pullback \(f^*\) is the left adjoint of the pushforward \(f_*\), and likewise for
2802	  \(h\) and \(h \mathbin{;} f\); the comparison \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) is the adjunction
2803	  \emph{mate} of the pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}\,f\)
2804	  (\cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}) across the pullback--pushforward adjunction,
2805	  and similarly for \(h\) and \(h \mathbin{;} f\). Since the mate correspondence is a
2806	  bijection, the asserted identity follows once both sides are transposed under the
2807	  adjunction \((h \mathbin{;} f)^* \dashv (h \mathbin{;} f)_*\).
2808	
2809	  Under this transpose the left-hand side \(\mathrm{pbu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\) becomes the
2810	  pushforward comparison \(\mathrm{upu}(h \mathbin{;} f)\). The right-hand side, a
2811	  composite involving \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}\) and the mates of
2812	  \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,h\), transposes --- using the conjugation of the
2813	  pseudofunctoriality isomorphisms (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f\) on the left adjoints
2814	  corresponds to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f\) on the right adjoints) and the unit of
2815	  the composite adjunction \((f^* \dashv f_*) \circ (h^* \dashv h_*)\) --- exactly into the
2816	  right-hand side of the pushforward coherence
2817	  \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, namely
2818	  \(\mathrm{upu}\,f \mathbin{;} f_*(\mathrm{upu}\,h) \mathbin{;}
2819	  (\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{hom}\). Thus the two transposed sides coincide
2820	  by \cref{lem:unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp}, and the original identity follows by the
2821	  injectivity of the transpose.

2836	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,Y.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf},

2844	\begin{proof}
2845	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
2846	  This is a one-step consequence of a Mathlib instance, with no chart-chase. The
2847	  canonical comparison map is the genuine Mathlib morphism
2848	  \[
2849	    \mathtt{SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,f :
2850	      f^*\mathcal{O}_X \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_Y,
2851	  \]
2852	  the unit comparison \(\eta\) of \(f^*\); abbreviate it \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\). Mathlib
2853	  proves this map is an isomorphism whenever the comparison functor
2854	  \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}\) on opens is \(\mathtt{Final}\): the instance
2855	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal}\).

2858	  morphism of schemes \(f\). The preimage functor \(\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}
2859	  : \mathtt{Opens}\,X \to \mathtt{Opens}\,Y\) is a frame homomorphism: it preserves
2860	  arbitrary unions and finite intersections, hence preserves all finite limits. A
2861	  functor between small categories that preserves finite limits is representably flat,
2862	  so \(\mathtt{final\_of\_representablyFlat}\) gives \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,f.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\)
2863	  with no hypothesis on \(f\) whatsoever. (There is no need to pass to affine charts:
2864	  finality is not a special feature of open immersions but a general property of
2865	  preimage-on-opens.)

2868	  directly, and \(\mathrm{pbu}\,f\) is an isomorphism for every \(f\). The asserted
2869	  isomorphism \(f^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) is the bundled iso
2870	  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) of this comparison.
2871	\end{proof}

386	
387	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
388	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
389	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
390	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
391	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
392	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
393	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
394	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
395	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
396	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
397	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
398	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
399	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
400	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
401	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
402	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
403	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
404	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
405	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
406	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
407	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
408	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
409	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
410	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
411	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
415	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
416	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
417	
418	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
419	
420	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
421	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
422	
423	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
424	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
425	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
426	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
427	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
428	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
429	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
430	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
431	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
432	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
433	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
434	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
435	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
436	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
437	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
438	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
439	      definitionally.
440	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
441	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
442	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
443	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
444	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
445	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
446	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
447	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
448	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
449	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
450	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
451	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
452	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
453	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
454	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
455	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
456	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
457	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
458	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
459	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
460	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
461	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
462	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
463	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
464	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
465	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
466	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
467	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
468	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
469	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
470	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
471	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
472	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
473	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
474	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
475	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
476	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
477	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
478	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
479	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
480	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
481	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
482	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
483	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
484	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
485	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
486	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
487	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
488	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
489	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
490	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
491	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
492	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
493	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
494	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
495	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
496	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
497	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
498	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
499	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
500	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
501	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
502	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
503	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
504	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
505	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
506	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
507	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
508	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
509	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
510	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
511	    intro U
512	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
513	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
514	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
515	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
516	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
517	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
518	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
519	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
520	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
521	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
522	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
523	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm
524	
525	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
526	
527	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
528	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
529	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
530	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
531	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
532	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
533	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
534	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
535	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
536	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
537	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
538	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
539	  intro x
540	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x

542	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
543	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
544	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
545	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
546	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
547	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
548	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
549	    tensorObj_unit_iso

13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
64	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
65	
66	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
67	`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
68	functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
69	this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
70	noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
71	    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
72	      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
73	  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
74	    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
75	  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
76	    dsimp
77	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
78	    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
79	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
80	    rfl)
81	
82	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
83	    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
84	  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable
85	
86	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
87	    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
88	  ext M
89	  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
90	  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
91	    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
92	  rintro (_ | _)
93	  all_goals
94	    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
95	      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)
96	
97	instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
98	  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
99	    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)
100	
101	end

109	  {G : D ⥤ E} {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)

111	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
112	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId.{v} S).symm
113

115	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
116	category of presheaves of modules. -/
117	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
118	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
119

127	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardComp.{v} φ ψ)
128

130	to the pullback for the composition. -/

132	    pullback.{v} φ ⋙ pullback.{v} ψ ≅
133	      pullback.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=

135	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} φ) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} ψ)
136	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ))
137	    (pushforwardComp φ ψ)

146	        pullbackComp.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) ψ' :=

module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Abelian
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Colimits
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Adjunction.Adj
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Adjunction.Cat
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Functor.LocallyDiscrete

/-!
# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme

In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe t u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}

variable (X) in
/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf

namespace Modules

/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N

instance : Category X.Modules where
  Hom := Modules.Hom
  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

section Functor

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

end Functor

variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}

section Presheaf

/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf

/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)

instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

variable (M) in
@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
  M.val.map_smul _ _ _

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  ext U x
  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M

@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :

end LaxMonoidal

section OplaxMonoidal
variable [G.LaxMonoidal]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The left adjoint of a lax monoidal functor is oplax monoidal. -/
@[simps -isSimp, implicit_reducible]
def leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : F.OplaxMonoidal where
  η := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm (ε G)
  δ X Y := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm ((adj.unit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.unit.app Y) ≫ μ G _ _)
  δ_natural_left _ _ := by
    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_left]
    simp [← tensorHom_id]
  δ_natural_right _ _ := by
    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_right]
    simp [← id_tensorHom]
  oplax_associativity X Y Z := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, assoc]
    conv_lhs =>
      rw [homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc, map_comp,
        ← μ_natural_left_assoc, map_comp, map_comp, tensorHom_def'_assoc]
      dsimp
      rw [← comp_whiskerRight_assoc]
    conv_rhs =>
      rw [← μ_natural_right, homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc,
        map_comp, tensorHom_def_assoc, ← associator_naturality_left_assoc]
      dsimp
      rw [← MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp_assoc, map_comp,
        unit_naturality_assoc, MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp,
        unit_naturality_assoc, right_triangle_components, comp_id, assoc,
        tensorHom_def, MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp_assoc,
        ← associator_naturality_middle_assoc, ← associator_naturality_right_assoc,
        ← associativity G, ← comp_whiskerRight_assoc, ← tensorHom_def,
        ← whisker_exchange_assoc, ← comp_whiskerRight_assoc]
    simp)
  oplax_left_unitality _ := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_left, ← tensorHom_id,
      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
    simp [tensorHom_def', homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])
  oplax_right_unitality _ := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_right, ← id_tensorHom,
      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
    simp [tensorHom_def, homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])

instance :
    letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
    adj.IsMonoidal := by
  letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
  refine ⟨?_, fun X Y ↦ ?_⟩
  · simp [homEquiv_counit, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η]
  · simp [homEquiv_counit, ← μ_natural, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ]

end OplaxMonoidal

attribute [local simp] leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ
  rightAdjointLaxMonoidal_ε rightAdjointLaxMonoidal_μ in
/-- If `F ⊣ G` is an adjunction, the `G` is lax monoidal iff `F` is oplax monoidal.
It is advisable to use `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` and
`Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`, because compatibilities between
the oplax monoidal left adjoint and the lax monoidal right adjoint
(`Adjunction.IsMonoidal`) have been stated for these definitions. -/
def laxMonoidalEquivOplaxMonoidal : G.LaxMonoidal ≃ F.OplaxMonoidal where
  toFun _ := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
  invFun _ := adj.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal
  left_inv _ := by
    ext
    · simp

=== pushforward (full) def ===
variable {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)

attribute [local simp] pushforward₀ in
/-- The pushforward functor `PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S` induced by
a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
@[simps! obj_obj]
noncomputable def pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S :=
  pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ

/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardCompToPresheaf :
    pushforward.{v} φ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
  Iso.refl _

lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
      (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl

/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_obj_map_apply`. -/
@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply' (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
      DFunLike.coe
        (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_]
          ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (S.map f).hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _)))
        (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl

lemma pushforward_map_app_apply {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
    (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl

/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_map_app_apply`. -/
@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app_apply' {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
    DFunLike.coe
      (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_] ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _))
      (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl

section

variable (R) in
/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
the identify functor of the category of presheaves of modules. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardId :
    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  Iso.refl _

section

variable {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G : D ⥤ E} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of presheaves of modules
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
  Iso.refl _

variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')

=== pushforward₀OfCommRingCat abbrev ===
/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category
of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
modules on `F.op ⋙ R`. -/
abbrev pushforward₀OfCommRingCat (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) :
    PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⥤
      PresheafOfModules.{v} ((F.op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ _ _) :=
  pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)

/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def pushforward₀CompToPresheaf (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
    pushforward₀.{v} F R ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
  Iso.refl _

variable {F}
variable {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)

namespace PresheafOfModules

variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D]
  (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u})

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in

  Functor.CoreMonoidal.toMonoidal
    { εIso := Iso.refl _
      μIso _ _ := Iso.refl _
      left_unitality _ := by rw [← cancel_epi (λ_ _).inv]; cat_disch
      right_unitality _ := by rw [← cancel_epi (ρ_ _).inv]; cat_disch }

end PresheafOfModules

  • `Scheme.Modules.pullback f = SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` and the
    underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` are BOTH defined as
    `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` — an ABSTRACT left adjoint with NO sectionwise and NO

    `pullback_obj` / `pullbackObjIso` / monoidal-`pullback` anywhere in

    via `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`; assemble the sectionwise tensorators `μ`" — cannot
    typecheck: there is no sectionwise `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj` to attach the
    `extendScalars` tensorator to.
  • `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` DOES give a comparison MAP `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶
    f^*A ⊗ f^*B` for free (from `pushforward` lax-monoidal), but only at the PRESHEAF
    level (no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)` exists). Proving `IsIso δ` still
    reduces, via `isIso_of_isIso_app`, to a SECTIONWISE identification of the abstract
    pullback with `extendScalars` — i.e. the same missing formula. Invertibility of `δ`
    is the genuine content (extension of scalars is strong; restriction is only lax), not
    derivable purely abstractly.
  • `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso only under `F.Final` (open
    immersions), false for general `f`.

RECOMMENDED PIVOT (do NOT re-dispatch the sectionwise-`extendScalars` recipe):
  (1) **Local-chart-finality route** (the trick that ALREADY proves
      `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` for GENERAL `f`, `LineBundlePullback.lean`

      relevant map factors through the LOCAL map `g = f.resLE U V` whose `Opens.map g.base`
      IS `Final` (`final_of_representablyFlat`), so `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g` is an iso.
      For `pullbackUnitIso` this reduces (probed live) to

  (3) **STRATEGIC alternative for the consumer**: the RPF structure maps are the

/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⟶ F.op ⋙ R₀` of presheaves of

is the composite of the strong-monoidal topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`
(Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal `restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`),
hence lax monoidal by `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
from which the abstract left-adjoint pullback inherits its oplax comparison `δ`
(`pullbackOplaxMonoidal`). Project-local supplement. -/
noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
  exact h

Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂) ⟶ (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂`
of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ` unfolds to
`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`. The hypothesis uses the *outer* `forget₂` association so the
`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` target and the `restrictScalars` source agree syntactically
(no monoidal-category diamond). This is the right-adjoint lax structure that an eventual
oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit. Project-local
supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
  exact h

input.new_string:
/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`
of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ` unfolds to
`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.

The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,
the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂` (the form
`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s target carries). The two are defeq; bridging them with a local
`MonoidalCategory` instance triggers a kernel-rejected diamond, so instead `φ` is defeq-cast
to the outer form (`φ'`) for the `restrictScalars` factor, and the resulting composite — defeq
to `pushforward φ` — is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
that an eventual oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.
Project-local supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
  exact h

/-- **The abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback is oplax monoidal**, with canonical
comparison map `δ_{A,B} : f^*(A ⊗ B) ⟶ f^*A ⊗ f^*B`. This is the mate of the lax
tensorator of `pushforward φ` (`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`) across the
pullback–pushforward adjunction, via Mathlib's doctrinal `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. It
supplies the canonical comparison map that the eventual `pullbackTensorIso` upgrades to an
isomorphism — note the map exists for the *abstract* left adjoint with no sectionwise value
(no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` is needed, contra the earlier reading: the comparison
lives at the presheaf level where `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal). Project-local supplement;
what remains Mathlib-absent is the concrete inverse-image model needed to prove `δ` is an
iso (see the Phase-2 note below). -/
noncomputable instance presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal
    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
    [(PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint] :
    (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).OplaxMonoidal :=
  (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (2, axiom-clean):**
  - `presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal` (instance, ~L1136) — `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`
    for CommRingCat-factored `φ`. The analogist-named missing ingredient `μ_G`.
  - `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` (instance, ~L1156) — `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).OplaxMonoidal`,
    the canonical comparison map `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶ f^*A ⊗ f^*B` at the presheaf level, via Mathlib's
    `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` on the lax pushforward.
- **Cleanup:** the stale HANDOFF comment block (old ~L1120–1172) — which falsely asserted
  `pullbackUnitIso` "NOT closable" / `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` iso "only for Final f" (both fixed iter-241)
  — replaced by an accurate Phase-2 status note naming the genuine blocker.
- **Declarations blocked (1):** `pullbackTensorIso` (blueprint `lem:pullback_tensor_iso`) and hence
  `IsInvertible.pullback` (`lem:isinvertible_pullback`) — blocked on a Mathlib-absent **concrete
  inverse-image functor** (Mathlib-scale, see below). NO sorry added.
- **Sorry count across file: 2 → 2** (unchanged; both pre-existing and FORBIDDEN this iter:
  L693 `exists_tensorObj_inverse`, L1202 `PicSharp` `addCommGroup` — untouched).
- File compiles, `lean_diagnostic_messages severity=error` → empty. Both new instances
  `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## `presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal` (~L1136) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ = pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`,
  composite of strong-monoidal `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib `PushforwardZeroMonoidal`) and
  lax-monoidal `restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` in `…/PresheafInternalHom.lean`),
  lax by `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.
- **KEY GOTCHA (forget₂-association diamond):** `pushforward`'s expected target type is the *inner*
  association `F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`, but the `MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules _)` instance and
  `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s target are keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂`.
  Bridging the two with a local `letI : MonoidalCategory …` triggers a kernel-rejected instance
  diamond (the `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` re-synthesizes a *different* middle `MonoidalCategory` than
  the `letI` one, so it cannot find the `restrictScalars φ` lax instance the `letI` carried).
  **FIX (reusable recipe):** keep the hypothesis in the inner form (so `pushforward φ` typechecks), then
  `let φ' : (S₀⋙forget₂) ⟶ (F.op⋙R₀)⋙forget₂ := φ` (a pure defeq cast to the outer form), build
  `(pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance` natively (no
  diamond — both factors now agree on the outer middle), and `exact` it back (defeq to `pushforward φ`).

## `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal` (~L1156) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Approach:** `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` (Mathlib
  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:1009`), consuming the lax pushforward above. Needs
  `[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]` as an instance arg (automatic for the SmallCategory/scheme case).
- **Correction to the iter-242 directive premise:** the directive (and old HANDOFF) claimed the oplax
  route "requires `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)`" which the project lacks. That is true only at the
  SHEAF level. At the **presheaf** level `PresheafOfModules` IS monoidal, the pullback is a genuine left
  adjoint, and `pushforward` is lax — so the canonical comparison `δ` DOES exist abstractly, with no
  sectionwise value and no `SheafOfModules` monoidal instance. The analogist's negative result still
  holds: `δ` is only a MAP; upgrading it to an ISO is the genuine remaining content.

## `pullbackTensorIso` / `IsInvertible.pullback` (NOT added) — genuine Mathlib-scale blocker
- **The blocker (confirmed by reading Mathlib source this iter):** the blueprint route is "build a concrete
  strong-monoidal `P ≅ pullback f`, transport via `leftAdjointUniq`." `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` and the
  underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` (abstract, no
  sectionwise value — `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`). For an OPEN
  immersion the concrete model was `pushforward β` (β a ring ISO), but for a GENERAL `f` the concrete left
  adjoint is the inverse image `(inverse-image presheaf) ⋙ extendScalars`:
  - The strong-monoidal `extendScalars` core (`AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange`) exists ONLY at
    `ModuleCat.extendScalars` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean:42`). There is
    **no** `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` in Mathlib.
  - The underlying-presheaf inverse image is a **left Kan extension** along `(Opens.map f.base).op`
    (a colimit, NOT sectionwise). No concrete inverse-image-of-presheaves-of-modules functor exists in
    Mathlib at the pin.
  Building that concrete strong-monoidal functor + its adjunction to `pushforward` + monoidal transport is
  the genuine multi-hundred-LOC, Mathlib-scale obligation. NOT closable in one session.
- **NEXT STEPS (for the planner / next prover), in order:**
  1. **Transport the presheaf `δ` (now landed, `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) to the SHEAF/substrate
     level** as a comparison MAP `(pullback f).obj (tensorObj M N) ⟶ tensorObj (pullback f M) (pullback f N)`,
     using `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback` (Steps 1–2 of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, valid for
     GENERAL f) + the landed `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (RHS reconciliation). This is a MAP, ~moderate LOC,
     axiom-clean-feasible, no concrete inverse image needed.
  2. **Upgrade that map to an iso for the `IsInvertible.pullback` consumer via the local-trivialization
     route** (avoids the concrete inverse image entirely): for the specific invertible pair `M, N`, trivialize
     locally (needs the forward bridge `IsInvertible M → IsLocallyTrivial M`, the deferred "easy direction"),
     pull back (locally `f^*M ≅ 𝒪`, `f^*N ≅ 𝒪`), so `δ` restricts to the canonical `𝒪⊗𝒪` comparison
     (iso); conclude `δ` iso globally by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (this file, axiom-clean). Then
     `IsInvertible.pullback` = `δ⁻¹ ≫ f^*e ≫ pullbackUnitIso` on witness `f^*N`. This route needs the
     forward bridge `IsInvertible ⇒ IsLocallyTrivial` built first (scheduled at A.2.c entry).
  3. **OR** the full general `pullbackTensorIso` for arbitrary `M,N` via the concrete inverse image (the
     Mathlib-scale build) — only needed if a non-invertible consumer ever appears (none on the A.2.c path).
- **Informal agent:** MOONSHOT key IS set this iter (the old "401" note is stale). Not called: the blocker
  is a precise Mathlib-API gap (concrete inverse-image functor absent), not a proof-sketch question.

## Blueprint marker flags (for review agent)
- `lem:pullback_tensor_iso`, `lem:isinvertible_pullback` — remain UNPROVEN (no `\leanok`); blocked as above.
- The two new instances (`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`, `presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`) are supporting
  infrastructure not currently named in the chapter; the plan agent may wish to add a
  `lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal` / `lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal` block under
  `sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality` and `\lean{}`-pin them (both axiom-clean, ready for `\leanok`).

## What I did

**Verdict: Partial progress — 2 axiom-clean declarations landed; primary target (`pullbackTensorIso`) blocked on a confirmed Mathlib-scale gap.**

### Landed (both axiom-clean: `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`)
1. **`presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`** (`TensorObjSubstrate.lean:1137`) — `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`, the analogist-named missing ingredient `μ_G`.
2. **`presheafPullbackOplaxMonoidal`** (`:1159`) — `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).OplaxMonoidal`, the **canonical comparison map** `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶ f^*A ⊗ f^*B` via Mathlib's `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`.

This **corrects the directive's premise**: the oplax `δ` does *not* need `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules)` (which the project lacks) — at the **presheaf** level `PresheafOfModules` is monoidal and the pullback is a genuine left adjoint, so `δ` exists abstractly. The analogist's negative result still stands: `δ` is only a *map*; upgrading it to an *iso* is the genuine content.

### Cleanup
Replaced the stale "NOT closable" HANDOFF block (factually wrong post‑iter‑241) with an accurate Phase‑2 status note.

### The genuine blocker (why `pullbackTensorIso`/`IsInvertible.pullback` are not closed)
I read the Mathlib source and confirmed: the blueprint's "concrete `P` ≅ pullback via `leftAdjointUniq`" route needs a **concrete inverse-image functor** for general `f`. Both halves are Mathlib-absent at the pin:
- no `PresheafOfModules.extendScalars` (the `distribBaseChange` strong-monoidal core exists only at `ModuleCat.extendScalars`);
- the topological inverse image is a **left Kan extension** along `(Opens.map f.base).op` (a colimit, not sectionwise).

Building that concrete strong-monoidal functor + adjunction is a multi-hundred-LOC, Mathlib-scale obligation — not closable in one session. (The open-immersion case `tensorObj_restrict_iso` escaped this only because its structure map is a ring *iso*.)

### Handoff (next steps, both avoid the concrete inverse image)
1. Transport the landed presheaf `δ` to the **sheaf/substrate** level as a map, via `sheafificationCompPullback` + the existing `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`.
2. For the `IsInvertible.pullback` consumer specifically, upgrade `δ` to an iso by **local trivialization** (needs the deferred forward bridge `IsInvertible ⇒ IsLocallyTrivial`) + the existing `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` — sidestepping the arbitrary-module general case entirely.

Full detail in `.archon/task_results/TensorObjSubstrate.md`. The file compiles with no errors; the only `sorry`s are the two pre-existing forbidden ones (untouched). A reusable gotcha (the `forget₂`-association kernel diamond and its defeq-cast fix) is recorded in memory.
